title: App Usage
intro: I designed an experience that helps Jira Data Center admins see where third‑party apps are used. I partnered with engineering on feasibility, ran cross‑functional workshops to define the MVP, and led customer research with admins and TAMs to validate workflows. I mapped end‑to‑end flows, including edge cases, and aligned stakeholders via DACIs. The app launched with a 4/4 rating and ~2,000 installs.
objective: To give administrators a way to monitor how their 3rd party marketplace apps are being used in Jira DC. The focus was to give them a sense of where apps are being used, rather than how they are being used.
approach: Worked closely with feature lead developers to understand what data that could be collected and presented to administrators.
Ran a workshop with the cross-functional team to explore what the MVP could looks like, what information we want to show and how frequently to sample data.
Validated high-level concepts with 3 Technical Account Managers as proxys for DC Admins before beginning the ‘make’ phase of the project.
Conducted research with 8 customers to validate proposed high-level designs and whether they met requirements around app usage, as well as to understand how customers approached the problem today.
Mapped the flow of installation and usage of plugin, as well as error states and empty states.
challenges: It was difficult to surface high-level insights that showed whether an app was being used or not, in a meaningful way.
outcome: Our app received 4/4 stars when we launched it and has ~2,000 installs to date.
